key_insight: The paper derives reliable a posteriori error bounds for stabilized finite element solutions of time-dependent mean field games by linking the error norm to the dual norm of the coupled PDE residual.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a practical way to certify and adaptively control FEM accuracy in coupled Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman and Kolmogorov-Fokker-Planck simulations.
method: Prove equivalence between the solution error norm and the dual residual norm for the coupled system.
Build a posteriori estimators from residual terms, temporal jump terms, and stabilization-term contributions.
Show that for mass-lumping in time and affine-preserving spatial stabilization, stabilization terms can be bounded by residual and jump estimators.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical derivations of reliable, efficient, and locally computable estimators, rather than numerical benchmarks.
watch_item: The improved estimator relies on specific stabilization choices, so its tightness and locality may not transfer directly to all stabilized FEM discretizations.
paper_title: A posteriori error bounds for finite element approximations of time-dependent mean field games
